entries.content: hello
Hi! How can I help you today?
what are the tools you can use clanka

I can use these tools here: readFile, search, writeFile, applyPatch, removeFile, renameFile, mkdir, ls, rg, glob, bash, gh, delegate, webSearch, fetchMarkdown, sleep, taskComplete. I can also explain which to pick for a specific task.
what tools would you like to have beyond these

Requested capabilities beyond current tools: AST/symbol code intelligence, structured test runner API, language server access, git hunk/staging primitives, issue/PR context fetcher, DB/schema inspection sandbox, browser automation trace replay, and secrets-safe env introspection.
ok implement them, follwow guidelines and best practices and effect v4 usage
Started repo exploration to identify where AgentTools and runtime wiring are implemented before making changes.
how could the tools themselves be like HMR so you can create -> test it without me restarting the binary or you rebuilding it dynamic hot reload for tools only?
did you get stuck on rg, do tool calls have a maxTimeout setup?

Explained design for in-process tool HMR: watched tool registry, dynamic import + validation, atomic swaps, per-run snapshot consistency, and toolsDts regeneration. Confirmed current timeout behavior: bash has timeout (default 120s), rg and gh do not; VM timeout does not cap async tool runtime.
what about making new tools available right as they're coded on the tui, can you wire that up, like tool-hmr
Confirmed feasibility and started reviewing AgentExecutor, Agent, TUI, and entrypoints for integration seams.
what about the TUI itself, can we run it in --watch mode so if you modify it i see the changes live?
Yes. Suggested Bun watch restart mode for TUI: bun --watch run bin/clanka --mock (or examples/tui.ts).
for the tui?
Yes, specifically for TUI. Auto-restarts process on file changes; state resets on each restart.
what about outside watch mode, real tool hmr for when non watch mode
Clarified true non-watch tool HMR requires in-process hot swap with registry + watcher, safe per-run snapshots, and TUI reload notifications.
ok implement it, also think about the same for the TUI/layout so its easy to modify for final users too
Continued design/codebase inspection for tool-HMR and layout-HMR integration points.
whats missing

Listed missing implementation items: hot registry services, watcher runtime, dynamic import+validation, atomic swap, executor/agent refactors, TUI layout plugin/watch support, tests/docs/changeset/validate.
implement all, fully test with  https://github.com/microsoft/tui-test also make yourself a tool using effect/Terminal.execute or run COmmand o rwhatever effect has so you can call the shell too
Began investigating Effect APIs for command execution and watch primitives in local effect-smol reference.
are we currently fully persisitng history into sessions for each project? we should, also resume by default from last session on current project when launching or relaunching
Answered no: current history and TUI state are in-memory; no per-project persisted session resume exists yet.
now do you have a write file tool? implement all create all test debug qa fix all
is there anyway you can try to save our current live session from memory into a jsonl file somehow
extract it all from your current context write it all down into a file
